codeReview.summary: Final verification feature completed successfully. Worker performed comprehensive end-to-end verification on a fresh clone including npm install, full test suite (264 tests), and release-check (skills validation, typecheck, tests, secret scan, pack check). All verification steps passed. No code changes were made by this feature as it is purely a verification step.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly used /tmp directory for fresh clone verification (not /Users/scrimwiggins/tmp or ~/tmp). AGENTS.md documents this pattern: 'Fresh clone test is performed to /tmp for isolation'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'cd /tmp && rm -rf fft_nano_fresh_test && git clone ... fft_nano_fresh_test'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used exact clone path from AGENTS.md: fft_nano_fresh_test. The naming convention 'fft_nano_fresh_<purpose>' is consistent with prior verification work (fft_nano_fresh_verify, fft_nano_val_repo_003).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md verification commands section shows fresh clone pattern. Worker used 'fft_nano_fresh_test' for final verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill (worker/SKILL.md) Final Verification section lists 'npm run secret-scan' in the procedure, but the worker didn't run it as a standalone command - secret-scan was run as part of 'npm run release-check' which includes it. This is acceptable as release-check is more comprehensive.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker skill says: 'npm install; npm run secret-scan; npm test; npm run release-check'. Worker ran: 'npm install; npm test; npm run release-check' (secret-scan is included in release-check). Transcript shows: 'Release check passed all stages: skills validation, typecheck, tests (264 pass), secret scan, pack content check'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill's Final Verification procedure lists commands but doesn't specify working directory context or timeout. Worker correctly inferred these from context (180s for install/tests, 120s for release-check).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill shows command list without timeout/directory. Worker transcript shows: 'timeout: 180' for npm install, 'timeout: 180' for npm test, 'timeout: 120' for npm run release-check. All commands used 'cd /tmp/fft_nano_fresh_test' context.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used npm run commands that are documented in .factory/services.yaml (install, test, release-check). All commands succeeded.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml shows: 'install: npm install', 'test: npm test', 'release-check: npm run release-check'. Worker ran all three successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker observed '2 vulnerabilities (audit available)' after npm install but correctly did not treat this as a blocking issue since it's only an audit warning, not a dependency installation failure. This is the correct interpretation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript observation field: 'npm install succeeded with 142 packages, 2 vulnerabilities (audit available)'. Worker marked feature as success.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that this feature doesn't create code changes - the commitId (48cc86d) in the handoff is the most recent commit on main (governance milestone), not a commit made by final-verification. The feature only updated validation-state.json (mission file, not tracked in repo).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId: '48cc86d'. Git log shows 48cc86d is 'val: add governance milestone user testing synthesis report (pass)'. Worker updated validation-state.json in mission directory (not a git-tracked file).

summary: Final verification feature PASSED. Worker successfully performed comprehensive end-to-end verification on a fresh clone of the repository. All 264 tests passed, npm install succeeded, and release-check passed all stages (skills validation, typecheck, tests, secret scan, pack content check). No blocking issues found. Worker followed procedure correctly with minor acceptable deviations (combined secret-scan into release-check rather than running separately). The repository is verified as ready for release.
